headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 release dominated the week, paired with a sharp rotation toward context parallelism and attribution techniques.
body: Anthropic shipped Claude Fable 5 alongside the Mythos family, with Fable 5 free on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans through June 22 before usage credits apply, and demos showed it autonomously playing Factorio, simulating the solar system from first principles, and building a browser-based CAD editor with its own AI copilot. On the technique side, context parallelism, attribution, and filter propagation all broke out at top momentum, with resource analysis, software engineering, training-free methods, percolation, and auto-scaling entering as new entrants. Infrastructure framing also moved, as RunPod's Flash pushed decorator-based GPU deployment with serverless autoscaling, and a separate argument held that RAG remains viable when retrieval is defined broadly across vector, full text, and filtered search.
